ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "datecreated" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the account is active */,
  "is_email_verified" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the email is verified */,
  "is_phone_verified" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the phone number is verified */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the account */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the account */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., administrator, moderator, user) */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role assignment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user-role assignment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user-role assignment was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "login_history" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each login event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "login_date" TEXT /* Date of the login event */,
  "login_time" TEXT /* Time of the login event */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user's device */,
  "logout_date" TEXT /* Date of the logout event */,
  "logout_time" TEXT /* Time of the logout event */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the login session */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the login event was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_login_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "password_reset" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each password reset request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "reset_date" TEXT /* Date of the password reset request */,
  "reset_time" TEXT /* Time of the password reset request */,
  "token" TEXT /* Token sent to the user for password reset */,
  "reset_completed" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the password reset was completed */,
  "reset_completion_date" TEXT /* Date when the password reset was completed */,
  "reset_completion_time" TEXT /* Time when the password reset was completed */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the password reset request was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_password_reset_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "account_settings" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each account setting */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the account setting (e.g., account_status, email_notification) */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the account setting (e.g., active, inactive, enabled, disabled) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the account setting was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the account setting was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_account_settings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "accountSuspensions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each suspended account */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "suspension_date" TEXT /* Date of account suspension */,
  "suspension_reason" TEXT /* Reason for account suspension */,
  "suspension_end_date" TEXT /* Date when the suspension ends */,
  "suspension_end_time" TEXT /* Time when the suspension ends */,
  "suspended_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who suspended the account */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the suspension was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_accountSuspensions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_verification" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each email verification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "verification_date" TEXT /* Date of email verification */,
  "verification_status" TEXT /* Status of email verification (e.g., pending, verified, failed) */,
  "verification_code" TEXT /* Verification code sent to the user */,
  "expires_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the verification code expires */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the verification request was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_verification_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "two_factor_auth" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each two-factor authentication setting */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "auth_method" TEXT /* Method of two-factor authentication (e.g., SMS, authenticator app) */,
  "auth_status" TEXT /* Status of two-factor authentication (e.g., enabled, disabled) */,
  "secret_key" TEXT /* Secret key used for two-factor authentication */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the two-factor authentication setting was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the two-factor authentication setting was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_two_factor_auth_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("id")
)
